page.title: Dubhe (licence-free) Charter Review: What It's Like Onboard in Marbella

data.meta_description: Read our honest Dubhe (licence-free) Marbella review: 8m, 5 pax, departs Puerto Banús. See what it's like onboard, pricing & how to book.
data.summary: An honest review of the Dubhe, an 8-metre licence-free boat in Marbella that departs Puerto Banús. It carries up to 5 passengers and is ideal for small groups wanting a simple, skipper-free day on the water.
key_facts: Licence-free: no boating licence required to drive
Ideal for couples or small families
Great for exploring Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés

faq.q: Do I need a licence to drive the Dubhe in Marbella?
faq.a: No, the Dubhe is licence-free. You can operate it without any boating licence or experience, making it perfect for beginners. A full safety briefing is given before departure.

faq.q: How many people can the Dubhe carry?
faq.a: The Dubhe has a maximum capacity of 5 passengers. This makes it ideal for couples, small families, or a group of friends looking for an intimate day out.

faq.q: What is included in the Dubhe charter price?
faq.a: Every Dubhe charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Pricing is on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: Where does the Dubhe depart from?
faq.a: The Dubhe departs from Puerto Banús, Marbella's famous marina. You'll meet the skipper at the port before heading out into the Mediterranean.

faq.q: Can I visit Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés on the Dubhe?
faq.a: Yes, the Dubhe is well-suited for visiting these nearby coves. Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés are just a short ride from Puerto Banús and offer crystal-clear water for swimming.

faq.q: Is there a minimum rental duration for the Dubhe?
faq.a: The standard minimum is 2 hours, though longer half-day and full-day charters are available. Contact us via WhatsApp for exact availability and pricing.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on the Dubhe?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. The boat already includes complimentary drinks and snacks, but there is no restriction on extras.

faq.q: How do I book the Dubhe?
faq.a: Booking is easy: send us a WhatsApp message with your preferred date and duration. We'll confirm availability and provide a quote. Payment is secure and straightforward.

data.body_html:
If you are looking for a small, simple, and licence-free boat in Marbella, the Dubhe is worth a close look. At 8 metres long and carrying a maximum of 5 passengers, it is the most compact and accessible vessel in our fleet at Boat Rental Marbella. This honest review covers what it is like onboard, where you can go, and whether it suits your trip.

What is the Dubhe?

The Dubhe is an 8-metre motorboat that departs from Puerto Banús. Unlike larger yachts in our fleet, it requires no boating licence to drive, making it a great entry-level option. It has an open layout with seating for five, a small sunpad area, and a basic bimini top for shade. There is no cabin or toilet, so it is strictly a day-boat for coastal cruising and swimming stops.

Who is the Dubhe for?

The Dubhe is ideal for couples, small families, or groups of friends who want a straightforward, no-fuss day on the water. It is not designed for parties or large groups — the 5-person limit keeps things intimate. Because it is licence-free, first-time boaters can take the helm after a short safety briefing. If you want to explore the coastline without the complexity of a big yacht, this is a solid choice.

What is it like onboard?

Onboard, the Dubhe feels compact but functional. The helm station is simple, with basic instrumentation and a single outboard engine. Seating is arranged around the stern and bow, and there is a small table for snacks and drinks. The sunpad at the bow is large enough for two people to lie down. Storage is limited, so pack light. The boat handles well in calm conditions and is easy to moor near coves.

Where can you go from Puerto Banús?

From Puerto Banús, the Dubhe can reach several popular spots within a short cruise. Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés are both less than 2 nautical miles away, offering clear water for swimming and snorkelling. Further south, you can head toward Estepona or north toward Marbella Marina. The boat’s shallow draft allows it to get close to beaches. For a longer trip, the Golden Mile coastline offers scenic views of La Concha mountain.

What is included in the charter?

Every Dubhe charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). The skipper handles navigation and safety, so you can relax. Unlike our larger boats, the Dubhe does not include a jet ski or water toys. Pricing is on request via WhatsApp — we do not list fixed hourly rates for this boat.

How does it compare to other boats in our fleet?

The Dubhe is the smallest and most affordable option in our fleet. By comparison, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) costs €749 for 2 hours and offers more space and comfort. The Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) is slightly shorter but similarly licence-free. If you need more passenger capacity, consider the Fairline Targa 12m (10 pax) or the Azimut 39 (11 pax). For the biggest experience, the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) is a luxury option. Browse our full fleet at Boat Rental Marbella to compare.

Pros and cons of the Dubhe

  • Pros: Licence-free, easy to drive, low cost, ideal for small groups, departs from Puerto Banús.
  • Cons: No cabin or toilet, limited storage, no water toys, 5-person max, no fixed hourly pricing online.

How to book the Dubhe

Booking the Dubhe is straightforward. Send us a WhatsApp message with your preferred date and duration. We will confirm availability and provide a quote. Payment is handled securely, and the skipper will meet you at Puerto Banús on the day. For more details on our full fleet, visit our boat rental page or check our blog for other reviews and guides.

Frequently asked questions

Do I need a licence to drive the Dubhe in Marbella?

No, the Dubhe is licence-free. You can operate it without any boating licence or experience, making it perfect for beginners. A full safety briefing is given before departure.

How many people can the Dubhe carry?

The Dubhe has a maximum capacity of 5 passengers. This makes it ideal for couples, small families, or a group of friends looking for an intimate day out.

What is included in the Dubhe charter price?

Every Dubhe charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Pricing is on request via WhatsApp.

Where does the Dubhe depart from?

The Dubhe departs from Puerto Banús, Marbella's famous marina. You'll meet the skipper at the port before heading out into the Mediterranean.

Can I visit Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés on the Dubhe?

Yes, the Dubhe is well-suited for visiting these nearby coves. Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés are just a short ride from Puerto Banús and offer crystal-clear water for swimming.

Is there a minimum rental duration for the Dubhe?

The standard minimum is 2 hours, though longer half-day and full-day charters are available. Contact us via WhatsApp for exact availability and pricing.

Can I bring my own food and drinks on the Dubhe?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. The boat already includes complimentary drinks and snacks, but there is no restriction on extras.

How do I book the Dubhe?

Booking is easy: send us a WhatsApp message with your preferred date and duration. We'll confirm availability and provide a quote. Payment is secure and straightforward.

For more honest reviews and guides, visit our blog or browse our full fleet.
